title: Apply attribute coding and produce an estimation-ready coded long CSV
summary: Transform choice-set data into a coded long-format CSV ready for model estimation. Applies attribute coding from a JSON spec and outputs one row per respondent-task-alternative combination.
description: Reads a choice-sets CSV (the output of `build-choice-sets`) along with a coding specification JSON, applies the coding (effects coding, dummy coding, thermometer, etc.) to each design attribute, and writes a coded long-format CSV ready for direct ingestion by the modelling plugins. Also writes a 'cookbook' JSON describing how each estimation-time column maps back to the original attribute and level — needed by `expand-coefficients` for human-readable reporting.
usage:
[["choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long"]]
choice_sets_csv = "<choice-sets.csv>"
task_metadata_csv = "<task-metadata.csv>"
coding_json = "<coding.json>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "pilot"
# optional:
alternative_position_name = "alternative_position"
add_best_position_attribute = false
add_worst_position_attribute = false
negate_worst_utilities = false

arguments.name: choice_sets_csv
arguments.description: Path to the choice-sets CSV produced by `build-choice-sets`. Must contain one row per (respondent, task, alternative) with `chosen_best` / `chosen_worst` indicators and the design attribute columns named in the coding spec.

arguments.name: task_metadata_csv
arguments.description: Path to the task-metadata CSV produced alongside the choice-sets file. Describes the task structure (items per task, best/worst layout, none-mode) and is used to construct the per-row response variable correctly.

arguments.name: coding_json
arguments.description: Path to the coding specification JSON. One entry per design attribute, declaring the coding type (`dummy`, `effects`, `interp_effects`, `thermometer`) and any holdout / base-level overrides. Attributes not listed in the spec are passed through unchanged.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for the coded-long CSV and its accompanying cookbook JSON. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: alternative_position_name
arguments.description: Name of the column to write for alternative position within a task (1-based slot index). Used together with the position-attribute toggles to enable position-effect terms in the model. Omit to skip the position column entirely.

arguments.name: add_best_position_attribute
arguments.description: When `true`, add a coded position attribute for best-side responses, so the model can absorb the slot-position effect on best picks. Effective only if `alternative_position_name` is also set.

arguments.name: add_worst_position_attribute
arguments.description: When `true`, add a coded position attribute for worst-side responses, capturing slot-position effects on worst picks separately from best picks.

arguments.name: negate_worst_utilities
arguments.description: When `true`, negate the design-attribute features for worst-side rows so that worst picks contribute with the opposite sign in the linear utility. This is the conventional way to fold best and worst observations into a single MNL likelihood; leave at the default if your downstream model handles best / worst separately.

details:
`build-estimation-long` is the third and final stage of the wrangling pipeline. It converts human-readable choice-set rows into the numeric long-format file that estimation kernels actually consume. Two things happen here: (1) every design attribute named in `coding_json` is replaced with one or more coded columns according to its declared coding type, and (2) the best / worst chosen flags are folded into a single response variable.

The coding spec is a JSON document with one entry per attribute. Each entry declares a coding type (e.g., `effects`, `dummy`, `interp_effects`, `thermometer`) and may declare a holdout or base level. Attributes not present in the spec are written to the output unchanged, so you can mix coded and pass-through columns freely.

`negate_worst_utilities` toggles the standard 'best minus worst' representation: when enabled, worst-side rows are written with negated feature columns so a single MNL fit can absorb both halves. The plugins in this monorepo (`model-mnl-lg`, `model-mnl-lc`, `model-mnl-hb-mh`) all assume this layout, so it's the right default for most users — but it's left off by default to keep the output explicitly opt-in.

The accompanying cookbook JSON is what `expand-coefficients` uses later to translate fitted coefficient names back into the original attribute / level form. Keep it alongside the coded CSV; without it, model output is much harder to read.
value:
On success, `out_dir` contains:

- The coded long-format CSV (one row per respondent-task-alternative, with coded columns for each attribute and a single response variable).
- A cookbook JSON describing how each coded column maps back to the original attribute and level.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: `negate_worst_utilities = true` is required when feeding the output into the bundled `model-mnl-lg`, `model-mnl-lc`, or `model-mnl-hb-mh` plugins on best-worst data. The default is `false` so that callers using a different downstream model are not surprised.
Position-effect terms require both `alternative_position_name` and the corresponding `add_*_position_attribute` toggle.
The cookbook JSON is needed by `expand-coefficients` later — keep it next to the coded CSV.

see_also.description: Previous stage — produces the choice-sets and task-metadata CSVs that this command consumes.
Inverse operation — translate fitted coefficients back to attribute / level form using the cookbook.
Aggregate MNL fit on the coded long output.
Latent class fit on the coded long output.
Hierarchical Bayes MH fit on the coded long output.

examples.title: Basic best-worst estimation build with negation
examples.body:
[["choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long"]]
choice_sets_csv = "./choice-sets/choice_sets.csv"
task_metadata_csv = "./choice-sets/task_metadata.csv"
coding_json = "./coding.json"
run_dir = "./estimation"
run_name = "pilot1"
negate_worst_utilities = true

examples.title: Add separate best / worst position effects
examples.body:
[["choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long"]]
choice_sets_csv = "./choice-sets/choice_sets.csv"
task_metadata_csv = "./choice-sets/task_metadata.csv"
coding_json = "./coding.json"
run_dir = "./estimation"
run_name = "pilot2"
alternative_position_name = "alternative_position"
add_best_position_attribute = true
add_worst_position_attribute = true
negate_worst_utilities = true

examples.title: Best-only study — no worst-side negation, no position effects

fields.name: task_metadata_csv
fields.description: Path to the task metadata CSV.

fields.name: coding_json
fields.description: Path to the coding specification JSON.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for the coded-long CSV.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: alternative_position_name
fields.description: Name of the alternative-position column in the output. Optional.

fields.name: add_best_position_attribute
fields.description: Add a coded position attribute for best-side responses. Default: false.

fields.name: add_worst_position_attribute
fields.description: Add a coded position attribute for worst-side responses. Default: false.

fields.name: negate_worst_utilities
fields.description: Negate features for worst-side rows so best/worst fold into one MNL. Default: false.

artifacts.title: Coded long-format estimation data
artifacts.description: The estimation-ready file. One row per respondent-task-alternative, with all design attributes converted to numeric codes (effects, dummy, or thermometer coding) and a single response variable. This is the direct input to the modelling plugins (model-mnl-lg, model-mnl-lc, model-mnl-hb-mh).

fields.name: (coded attribute columns)
fields.description: One or more numeric columns per design attribute, encoded per the coding specification.

fields.name: y
fields.description: Response variable — positive for the chosen alternative, 0 for all other alternatives in the same task.

artifacts.title: Coding cookbook
artifacts.description: Maps every coded column name back to its original attribute and level. Required by expand-coefficients to translate model coefficients back into human-readable attribute/level form for reporting and charts.
